name: Zigbee Component
description: Zigbee is a low-power, mesh-based networking standard designed for IoT devices. Its low energy consumption makes it well-suited for battery-powered smart-home devices, while its limited bandwidth is ideal for devices that exchange only small amounts of data, such as switches, sensors, and motion detectors.

config_entries.label: Model
config_entries.description: Model Identifier of the Zigbee device. Defaults to the ESPHome node name.

config_entries.label: ID
config_entries.description: The ID to use for this `zigbee` component.

config_entries.label: Ieee802154 Vendor Oui
config_entries.description: Sets the Vendor Organizationally Unique Identifier (OUI). This allows replacing Nordic Semiconductor's default company ID with your own. The value must be a 24-bit integer in the range `0x000000` to `0xFFFFFF`. Alternatively, set to `random` to generate a new random OUI on every firmware compilation. This is useful during development to force the coordinator (ZHA/Z2M) to recognize the device as new after firmware updates.

config_entries.label: Power Source
config_entries.description: Indicates what kind of power the device uses. Affects sleep behavior. One of `UNKNOWN`, `MAINS_SINGLE_PHASE`, `MAINS_THREE_PHASE`, `BATTERY`, `DC_SOURCE`, `EMERGENCY_MAINS_CONST`, or `EMERGENCY_MAINS_TRANSF`. Defaults to `DC_SOURCE`.

config_entries.label: Router
config_entries.description: If true the Zigbee role will be `router` instead of `end device` and it will forward Zigbee packets from other devices. Routers drain more power than end devices and need to be always on for a stable Zigbee network. For this reasons battery-powered devices should not be used as routers. Defaults to `false`.

config_entries.label: Sleepy
config_entries.description: Disable RX during idle to reduce power consumption for battery-powered devices. It may cause stability problems with the Zigbee connection. Defaults to `false`.

config_entries.label: Wipe On Boot
config_entries.description: Erases all non-volatile memory data on boot, including Zigbee network pairing and preferences (e.g., last switch state). One of:

options.label: Erase data only on first boot after flashing new firmware, then preserve.
